# ============================================================================
# Forecast del ETF SPY (State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust)
# Serie de Tiempo para el próximo mes
# ============================================================================

# Instalación de paquetes necesarios (ejecutar una sola vez)
# install.packages(c("quantmod", "forecast", "ggplot2", "tseries", "TTR"))

# Cargar librerías
library(quantmod)
library(forecast)
library(ggplot2)
library(tseries)
library(TTR)

# ============================================================================
# 1. DESCARGA DE DATOS HISTÓRICOS
# ============================================================================

# Descargar datos del SPY de Yahoo Finance
# Últimos 2 años de datos para mejor análisis
getSymbols("SPY", from = "2024-03-20", to = "2026-03-20", periodicity = "daily")

# Extraer el precio de cierre ajustado
spy_prices <- Ad(SPY)  # Ad() = Adjusted Close
spy_prices <- na.omit(spy_prices)

# Convertir a serie de tiempo
spy_ts <- ts(as.numeric(spy_prices), frequency = 252)  # 252 días hábiles por año

# ============================================================================
# 2. ANÁLISIS EXPLORATORIO DE LA SERIE
# ============================================================================

# Visualizar la serie de tiempo
plot(spy_ts, main = "Histórico de Precios SPY (2024-2026)",
     xlab = "Tiempo", ylab = "Precio ($)", col = "steelblue", lwd = 1.5)
grid()

# Gráfico con ggplot2 (más elegante)
spy_df <- data.frame(
  Date = index(spy_prices),
  Price = as.numeric(spy_prices)
)

ggplot(spy_df, aes(x = Date, y = Price)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", size = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Precio del ETF SPY (2024-2026)",
       subtitle = "State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust",
       x = "Fecha", y = "Precio ($)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))

# Análisis de descomposición (tendencia, estacionalidad, ruido)
spy_decomposed <- decompose(spy_ts, type = "multiplicative")
plot(spy_decomposed)

# Prueba de estacionariedad (Augmented Dickey-Fuller)
cat("\n=== Prueba ADF (Augmented Dickey-Fuller) ===\n")
adf_test <- adf.test(spy_ts)
print(adf_test)

# Si no es estacionaria, diferenciar
if (adf_test$p.value > 0.05) {
  cat("\nLa serie NO es estacionaria. Aplicando diferenciación...\n")
  spy_diff <- diff(spy_ts)
  adf_test_diff <- adf.test(spy_diff)
  print(adf_test_diff)
}

# ============================================================================
# 3. ANÁLISIS ACF y PACF
# ============================================================================

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
acf(spy_ts, main = "ACF - SPY")
pacf(spy_ts, main = "PACF - SPY")
acf(diff(spy_ts), main = "ACF - SPY Diferenciada")
pacf(diff(spy_ts), main = "PACF - SPY Diferenciada")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# ============================================================================
# 4. MODELOS DE FORECASTING
# ============================================================================

# Período de pronóstico: 21 días hábiles (aproximadamente 1 mes)
forecast_horizon <- 21

# --- MÉTODO 1: Auto ARIMA ---
cat("\n=== MÉTODO 1: Auto ARIMA ===\n")
model_auto_arima <- auto.arima(spy_ts, seasonal = FALSE)
print(summary(model_auto_arima))
forecast_arima <- forecast(model_auto_arima, h = forecast_horizon)

# --- MÉTODO 2: Exponential Smoothing (ETS) ---
cat("\n=== MÉTODO 2: Exponential Smoothing (ETS) ===\n")
model_ets <- ets(spy_ts)
print(summary(model_ets))
forecast_ets <- forecast(model_ets, h = forecast_horizon)

# --- MÉTODO 3: Modelo de Regresión con Tendencia ---
cat("\n=== MÉTODO 3: Regresión con Tendencia ===\n")
time_index <- 1:length(spy_ts)
model_lm <- lm(spy_ts ~ time_index)
print(summary(model_lm))

# Predicción con el modelo lineal
future_time <- (length(spy_ts) + 1):(length(spy_ts) + forecast_horizon)
forecast_lm <- predict(model_lm, newdata = data.frame(time_index = future_time),
                       se.fit = TRUE, interval = "prediction", level = 0.95)

# --- MÉTODO 4: Promedio Móvil Suavizado ---
cat("\n=== MÉTODO 4: Suavizamiento de Holt-Winters ===\n")
model_hw <- HoltWinters(spy_ts)
print(summary(model_hw))
forecast_hw <- forecast(model_hw, h = forecast_horizon)

# ============================================================================
# 5. VISUALIZACIÓN DE PRONÓSTICOS
# ============================================================================

# Gráfico ARIMA
plot(forecast_arima, main = "Forecast SPY - ARIMA",
     xlab = "Tiempo", ylab = "Precio ($)")
grid()

# Gráfico ETS
plot(forecast_ets, main = "Forecast SPY - Exponential Smoothing",
     xlab = "Tiempo", ylab = "Precio ($)")
grid()

# Gráfico Holt-Winters
plot(forecast_hw, main = "Forecast SPY - Holt-Winters",
     xlab = "Tiempo", ylab = "Precio ($)")
grid()

# ============================================================================
# 6. RESUMEN DE PRONÓSTICOS
# ============================================================================

cat("\n" %+% strrep("=", 60) %+% "\n")
cat("RESUMEN DE PRONÓSTICOS PARA EL PRÓXIMO MES (21 DÍAS HÁBILES)\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60) %+% "\n")

# Obtener el último precio
last_price <- as.numeric(tail(spy_prices, 1))
cat("\nÚltimo precio registrado:", round(last_price, 2), "$\n")

# ARIMA
cat("\n--- ARIMA ---\n")
cat("Pronóstico puntual:", round(tail(forecast_arima$mean, 1), 2), "$\n")
cat("Límite inferior (95%):", round(tail(forecast_arima$lower[, 2], 1), 2), "$\n")
cat("Límite superior (95%):", round(tail(forecast_arima$upper[, 2], 1), 2), "$\n")
change_arima <- ((tail(forecast_arima$mean, 1) - last_price) / last_price) * 100
cat("Cambio esperado:", round(change_arima, 2), "%\n")

# ETS
cat("\n--- Exponential Smoothing (ETS) ---\n")
cat("Pronóstico puntual:", round(tail(forecast_ets$mean, 1), 2), "$\n")
cat("Límite inferior (95%):", round(tail(forecast_ets$lower[, 2], 1), 2), "$\n")
cat("Límite superior (95%):", round(tail(forecast_ets$upper[, 2], 1), 2), "$\n")
change_ets <- ((tail(forecast_ets$mean, 1) - last_price) / last_price) * 100
cat("Cambio esperado:", round(change_ets, 2), "%\n")

# Holt-Winters
cat("\n--- Holt-Winters ---\n")
cat("Pronóstico puntual:", round(tail(forecast_hw$mean, 1), 2), "$\n")
cat("Límite inferior (95%):", round(tail(forecast_hw$lower[, 2], 1), 2), "$\n")
cat("Límite superior (95%):", round(tail(forecast_hw$upper[, 2], 1), 2), "$\n")
change_hw <- ((tail(forecast_hw$mean, 1) - last_price) / last_price) * 100
cat("Cambio esperado:", round(change_hw, 2), "%\n")

# ============================================================================
# 7. EVALUACIÓN DE MODELOS (Backtest)
# ============================================================================

cat("\n" %+% strrep("=", 60) %+% "\n")
cat("EVALUACIÓN DE MODELOS\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60) %+% "\n")

# Calcular métricas de error
cat("\n--- Métricas ARIMA ---\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(sqrt(mean(forecast_arima$residuals^2)), 4), "\n")
cat("MAE:", round(mean(abs(forecast_arima$residuals)), 4), "\n")
cat("MAPE:", round(mean(abs(forecast_arima$residuals / spy_ts)) * 100, 4), "%\n")

cat("\n--- Métricas ETS ---\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(sqrt(mean(forecast_ets$residuals^2)), 4), "\n")
cat("MAE:", round(mean(abs(forecast_ets$residuals)), 4), "\n")
cat("MAPE:", round(mean(abs(forecast_ets$residuals / spy_ts)) * 100, 4), "%\n")

# ============================================================================
# 8. GRÁFICO COMPARATIVO
# ============================================================================

# Crear un gráfico con todos los pronósticos
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(forecast_arima, main = "ARIMA", ylab = "Precio ($)")
grid()

plot(forecast_ets, main = "Exponential Smoothing", ylab = "Precio ($)")
grid()

plot(forecast_hw, main = "Holt-Winters", ylab = "Precio ($)")
grid()

# Gráfico de residuales ARIMA
plot(forecast_arima$residuals, main = "Residuales ARIMA",
     ylab = "Residual", type = "l")
grid()
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# ============================================================================
# 9. CREAR TABLA DE PRONÓSTICOS
# ============================================================================

# Crear tabla con los pronósticos
forecast_table <- data.frame(
  Day = 1:forecast_horizon,
  ARIMA = round(as.numeric(forecast_arima$mean), 2),
  ETS = round(as.numeric(forecast_ets$mean), 2),
  HoltWinters = round(as.numeric(forecast_hw$mean), 2)
)

cat("\n" %+% strrep("=", 60) %+% "\n")
cat("TABLA DE PRONÓSTICOS - PRÓXIMO MES\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60) %+% "\n")
print(forecast_table)

# ============================================================================
# 10. EXPORTAR RESULTADOS
# ============================================================================

# Guardar tabla de pronósticos a CSV
write.csv(forecast_table, "spy_forecast.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n✓ Tabla de pronósticos guardada en 'spy_forecast.csv'\n")

# Guardar la sesión
save.image("spy_forecast_analysis.RData")
cat("✓ Análisis completo guardado en 'spy_forecast_analysis.RData'\n")
